---
title: "Iris Dataset Analysis"
date: 2025-10-04
categories: [Python, Machine Learning, Classification]
image: "https://images.pexels.com/photos/132476/pexels-photo-132476.jpeg"
description: "Exploratory data analysis and classification of the classic Iris dataset using statistical and machine learning models."
---

![Iris flower](https://images.pexels.com/photos/132476/pexels-photo-132476.jpeg){fig-align="center" width=100%}

*Photo: [Anthony 🙂](https://www.pexels.com/sk-sk/@inspiredimages/) — Pexels*

## Problem
Analyze the Iris dataset and evaluate classification models for predicting Iris species from flower measurements.

## Data
The **Iris dataset** is a classic dataset in statistics and machine learning, introduced in 1936.  
It contains **150 samples** from three Iris species, with **50 observations per species**:

- *Iris setosa*
- *Iris versicolor*
- *Iris virginica*

## Method
Exploratory data analysis included **histograms, scatter plots, and violin plots**.  
Three classification models were applied:

- Linear Discriminant Analysis
- Logistic Regression
- Decision Tree Classifier

## Tools
Python, pandas, NumPy, matplotlib, scikit-learn

## Result
Logistic Regression and Linear Discriminant Analysis achieved near-perfect classification performance.  
The Decision Tree model was more interpretable but slightly less stable and less accurate.

## Full Analysis
[Open the full notebook](../notebooks/iris_analysis.html)
